sourceMovie.title: Flashdance
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Flashdance for the same reason it broke through in 1983: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a romance rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Adrian Lyne keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Step Up
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Flashdance clicked for you, Step Up (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: High School Musical 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Flashdance clicked for you, High School Musical 2 (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Flashdance?
faq.answer: Try Step Up, High School Musical 2, Footloose—each shares DNA with Flashdance while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Flashdance?
faq.answer: Flashdance (1983) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Flashdance worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
